items.name: openagreements-restrictive-covenant-wyoming
items.description: Wyoming-specific employee restrictive covenant agreement with modular covenant structure (non-compete, non-solicitation, non-dealing, non-investment, confidentiality, non-disparagement). Incorporates Wyo. Stat. section 1-23-108 (SF 107, 2025) pathway gating for non-compete-adjacent covenants. Drafted as a starting point for licensed counsel review.
items.attribution_text: Authored by OpenAgreements contributors. Wyoming-specific analysis informed by publicly available commentary from Am Law firms including Ogletree Deakins, Fisher Phillips, Littler Mendelson, Foley & Lardner, Faegre Drinker, and Vinson & Elkins. See practice note for sources. Licensed under CC BY 4.0.

fields.name: employer_name
fields.description: Legal name of the employer

fields.name: employee_name
fields.description: Full legal name of the employee

fields.name: employee_title
fields.description: Employee job title or position (optional)

fields.name: effective_date
fields.description: Effective date of this agreement

fields.name: governing_law
fields.description: Governing law state

fields.name: worker_category
fields.description: Employee role classification under Wyo. Stat. section 1-23-108. AI-only field that drives memo warnings. Not shown in the output document.

fields.name: restriction_pathways
fields.description: Wyoming restriction pathway(s) under Wyo. Stat. section 1-23-108. AI-only field that drives memo warnings and conditional gating. Not shown in the output document. Options: Executive or Management Personnel, Trade Secret Protection, None.

fields.name: confidentiality_trade_secret_duration
fields.description: Duration of confidentiality obligations for trade secrets

fields.name: confidentiality_other_duration
fields.description: Duration of confidentiality obligations for non-trade-secret information
fields.default_value_rationale: 24 months is common for non-trade-secret confidentiality obligations in employment agreements.

fields.name: employee_nonsolicit_included
fields.description: Whether the employee non-solicitation covenant is included

fields.name: employee_nonsolicit_duration
fields.description: Duration of employee non-solicitation restriction
fields.default_value_rationale: 12 months is the most common enforceable duration. Mayer Brown guidance states 1-2 years is the outer range of reasonableness.

fields.name: covered_employee_period
fields.description: Lookback period for determining which employees are covered by the non-solicitation restriction.
fields.default_value_rationale: 12 months is the most common lookback period for employee non-solicitation scope.

fields.name: customer_nonsolicit_included
fields.description: Whether the customer/partner non-solicitation covenant is included

fields.name: customer_nonsolicit_duration
fields.description: Duration of customer/partner non-solicitation restriction
fields.default_value_rationale: 12 months is the most common enforceable duration. Compass Minerals uses 12 months for non-CEO employees and 24 months for CEO.

fields.name: covered_customer_period
fields.description: Lookback period for determining which customers are covered by the non-solicitation and non-dealing restrictions.
fields.default_value_rationale: 12 months is the most common lookback period for customer non-solicitation scope.

fields.name: noncompete_included
fields.description: Whether the non-competition covenant is included. Requires a lawful restriction pathway under Wyo. Stat. section 1-23-108.
fields.default_value_rationale: Wyo. Stat. section 1-23-108 voids most non-compete covenants. The drafter must affirmatively include this covenant only when a statutory pathway applies.

fields.name: noncompete_duration
fields.description: Duration of non-competition restriction
fields.default_value_rationale: 12 months is the most common enforceable duration. Hassler v. Circle C Resources (2022 WY 28) struck down a 24-month provision with broad scope. No Wyoming court has upheld a non-compete exceeding 24 months.

fields.name: territory
fields.description: Geographic scope of the non-competition restriction
fields.default: the geographic area in which Employee provided services
fields.default_value_rationale: Tied to the employee's actual service area. Narrower scope improves enforceability under Wyoming's strict scrutiny.

fields.name: competitive_business_definition
fields.description: Description of the business activities that constitute competition with the employer.

fields.name: specified_competitors
fields.description: Optional named list of specific competitors. Narrowing the restriction to named competitors strengthens enforceability.

fields.name: nondealing_included
fields.description: Whether the no-business-with-covered-customers covenant is included. Requires a lawful restriction pathway under Wyo. Stat. section 1-23-108.

fields.name: nondealing_duration
fields.description: Duration of the no-business-with-covered-customers restriction

fields.name: passive_public_holdings_threshold
fields.description: Maximum ownership percentage of publicly traded securities permitted under the Passive Public Holdings carveout.
fields.default_value_rationale: Five percent of any class of publicly traded securities is the standard passive investment carveout across Fortune 500 restrictive covenant agreements.

fields.name: noninvestment_included
fields.description: Whether the non-investment covenant is included. Requires a lawful restriction pathway under Wyo. Stat. section 1-23-108.

fields.name: noninvestment_duration
fields.description: Duration of non-investment restriction

fields.name: nondisparagement_duration
fields.description: Duration of non-disparagement obligation (measured from termination)
fields.default_value_rationale: 24 months is common for employment non-disparagement provisions.

fields.name: cloud_drive_id
fields.description: Optional document-system URI or file ID for execution copy traceability

fields.name: cloud_drive_id_footer
fields.description: Internal computed footer text for document-system traceability
